They had a few good ideas, but they look poorly executed for cheap laughs for an audience that gets bored after one minute without action and cheap VFX (I have nothing against good VFX).
Since I'm German I also got the trailer in German, which gives the movie even more C to Z movie quality. They really had a few good ideas, but nope 🗑️.

textDisplay:
Saw early pics of JK jacked, title Red One, was stoked for a RED prequel!

Then, saw promo pics with The Rock and was confused about this sequel to Red Notice

Now, I see Jumanji WttJ as the other listed credit on screen, hella cliched lines from the 90s (the naughty list is is VERY real, also ‘that was …. disturbing’ is interchangeable with ‘so THAT just happened’)

So I can only assume my kids are gonna love it and it’s gonna be charming as fk and I’ll begrudgingly watch it the first time, but enjoy it every. Single. Other. Time. It’ll be put on

Yeah, that’s sarcasm about the regularity that these things hit our house

But I’m 100% sincere this’ll probably be another mainstay in out house once it’s streaming, and that I’ll enjoy the fk out of it, no matter how cliche the trailers looking
textOriginal:
Saw early pics of JK jacked, title Red One, was stoked for a RED prequel!

Then, saw promo pics with The Rock and was confused about this sequel to Red Notice

Now, I see Jumanji WttJ as the other listed credit on screen, hella cliched lines from the 90s (the naughty list is is VERY real, also ‘that was …. disturbing’ is interchangeable with ‘so THAT just happened’)

So I can only assume my kids are gonna love it and it’s gonna be charming as fk and I’ll begrudgingly watch it the first time, but enjoy it every. Single. Other. Time. It’ll be put on

Yeah, that’s sarcasm about the regularity that these things hit our house

But I’m 100% sincere this’ll probably be another mainstay in out house once it’s streaming, and that I’ll enjoy the fk out of it, no matter how cliche the trailers looking

textDisplay:
It sadly is the way hollywood is happening right now. They feature as much of the A-list talent in every scene as possible to keep the viewer entertained. It's not even about the story at that point, it's about the actors and how much screen time they can squeeze in a trailer to pre-sell tickets. Even then, the movie could be really bad, but still make a lot of money from trailers like this. 

I agree with you, and think that trailers should only hit 30 seconds and make people hit the movie theater for STORY, not who is IN the movie.
textOriginal:
It sadly is the way hollywood is happening right now. They feature as much of the A-list talent in every scene as possible to keep the viewer entertained. It's not even about the story at that point, it's about the actors and how much screen time they can squeeze in a trailer to pre-sell tickets. Even then, the movie could be really bad, but still make a lot of money from trailers like this. 

I agree with you, and think that trailers should only hit 30 seconds and make people hit the movie theater for STORY, not who is IN the movie.

textDisplay: Am I the only person sick of these “Santa-action-hero” franchises? Not saying that as a hater but I’m honestly despising more and more this constant need for action and guns and beat ups it really kills of the beauty of Christmas. There’s two films in my mind that go down as the greatest Christmas movies ever made which are Krampus (2015) and the Polar Express which both holds that reverence towards Christmas and the importance of hope and joy. The St Nicolas in Polar Express feels ethereal and like there’s so much mystery and magic surrounding him which can’t and shouldn’t be explained away. He isn’t shown in Krampus but you have a sense that he’s full of wonder and enchantment as opposed to Krampus who brings chaos and fear and death.
textOriginal: Am I the only person sick of these “Santa-action-hero” franchises? Not saying that as a hater but I’m honestly despising more and more this constant need for action and guns and beat ups it really kills of the beauty of Christmas. There’s two films in my mind that go down as the greatest Christmas movies ever made which are Krampus (2015) and the Polar Express which both holds that reverence towards Christmas and the importance of hope and joy. The St Nicolas in Polar Express feels ethereal and like there’s so much mystery and magic surrounding him which can’t and shouldn’t be explained away. He isn’t shown in Krampus but you have a sense that he’s full of wonder and enchantment as opposed to Krampus who brings chaos and fear and death.

textDisplay:
Two things:

1) interesting concept and it looks like it’s trying to start a Live-Action Rise of the Guardians-type of universe like what Violent Night looked to do. 

2) Dwayne Johnson is still basically playing himself but Chris Evans has genuinely been looking like he’s actually out there now playing characters on a script rather than seeming like he’s still Captain America. But that’s only because the man is an actor who has range. From Romantic Comedies such as “Not Another Teen Movie”, to Drama/Thrillers like “Snowpiercer” and “Push”, to his more fun roles like “The Gray Man”.
textOriginal:
Two things:

1) interesting concept and it looks like it’s trying to start a Live-Action Rise of the Guardians-type of universe like what Violent Night looked to do. 

2) Dwayne Johnson is still basically playing himself but Chris Evans has genuinely been looking like he’s actually out there now playing characters on a script rather than seeming like he’s still Captain America. But that’s only because the man is an actor who has range. From Romantic Comedies such as “Not Another Teen Movie”, to Drama/Thrillers like “Snowpiercer” and “Push”, to his more fun roles like “The Gray Man”.

textDisplay: At the moment, the last film starring Dwayne Johnson is superhero blockbuster by Jaume Collet-Serra Black Adam, which was released in October 2022.  And in November 2024, Dwayne The Rock will return to the film industry again with new action comedy film by Jake Kasdan Red One.  What makes this film even more interesting is that Dwayne Johnson will be accompanied by Chris Evans and J.K. Simmons.  I will watch this film with great pleasure.
textOriginal: At the moment, the last film starring Dwayne Johnson is superhero blockbuster by Jaume Collet-Serra Black Adam, which was released in October 2022.  And in November 2024, Dwayne The Rock will return to the film industry again with new action comedy film by Jake Kasdan Red One.  What makes this film even more interesting is that Dwayne Johnson will be accompanied by Chris Evans and J.K. Simmons.  I will watch this film with great pleasure.
